title: linaria amethystea f. subalpina
intro: Linaria amethystea f. subalpina, also known as the dwarf toadflax or alpine toadflax, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Linaria amethystea f. subalpina include relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparation of the plant is typically made into a tea or infusion by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water, or by using it as a tincture or powder in traditional medicine.
